title: German Chicken Schnitzel
description: A classic German dish featuring breaded and fried chicken, served with a side of creamy sauce.
instructions: Prepare the breading station by placing flour in one shallow dish, eggs in another, and bread crumbs in a third.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Dip the chicken in the flour, coating both sides.
Dip the floured chicken in the eggs, making sure to coat completely.
Dip the egg-coated chicken in the bread crumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the breaded chicken to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
Transfer the cooked chicken to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
Reduce heat to medium and add the butter to the skillet.
Once melted, add the heavy cream and lemon juice to the skillet.
Stir to combine and simmer for 2-3 minutes or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Serve the chicken with the creamy sauce spooned over the top.
